name: English

motifs.name: Koru
motifs.meaning: New life, unfurling potential, the origin point of all things. Something is beginning — or being prevented from beginning.

motifs.name: Pa Tuwatawata
motifs.meaning: The fortified palisade — collective protection, sovereignty over space and governance.

motifs.name: Niho Taniwha
motifs.meaning: The taniwha's teeth — the colonial bite, extraction without consent, harm that must be named.

motifs.name: Kowhaiwhai
motifs.meaning: The law written on the rafter. A clash between tikanga and statute, ancestral authority and state power.

motifs.name: Unaunahi
motifs.meaning: Fish scales interlocking. This event calls for collective response — together they form armour.

motifs.name: Takarangi
motifs.meaning: The double spiral — history repeating, past and future wound together.

motifs.name: Pikorua
motifs.meaning: The double twist — two lives, two cultures, forever joined. Paths that separate always return. The symbol of inter-tribal gift, solidarity, eternal bond.

motifs.name: Manaia
motifs.meaning: The spiritual guardian — messenger between the living world and the spirit realm. Protector. The spiritual double of a person. Keeper of balance between seen and unseen.

motifs.name: Mangopare
motifs.meaning: The hammerhead shark — courage, determination, strength. The fighter who does not turn back. Warrior resolve.

motifs.name: Niho Mano
motifs.meaning: Strength, protection, guidance and ferocity. The shark is a revered guardian spirit (aitu) in Polynesian cosmology. In the pe'a and malu tattoos, this motif signals warrior readiness and the wearer's

motifs.name: Atualoa
motifs.meaning: Strength, endurance, and commitment to extended family (aiga). The centipede is venomous and symbolises danger and power; it also represents a creature that tends to its many offspring, standing for f

motifs.name: Fetu
motifs.meaning: Navigation, hope, and spiritual connection to ancestors and the heavens. Stars guided Polynesian voyagers across the Pacific and symbolise divine guidance. In tattooing, fetu marks the wearer's ancest

motifs.name: Malu Diamond
motifs.meaning: Protection and shelter. The entire female tattoo takes its name from this motif. 'Malu' means 'to protect, to shelter' in Samoan. The motif signifies the woman's role as keeper of heritage and protect

motifs.name: Aso
motifs.meaning: Shelter, protection, family, and home. The 'aso represents the structural rafters of the traditional oval Samoan house (fale). Carrying this motif signals devotion to family (aiga) and communal life.

motifs.name: Fa A Aupega
motifs.meaning: Community interdependence, collective effort, and the practice of fishing. The net (upega) motif symbolises traditional nets for catching sea turtles and pigeons. In tattooing, the net pattern relates

motifs.name: Pea
motifs.meaning: Sacred rite of passage marking Samoan manhood, social standing, and covenant with ancestors. 'Pe'a' means the Samoan flying fox. The composition is a living genealogical record: each motif band narrat

motifs.name: Va A Waka
motifs.meaning: Voyaging, ancestry, freedom, and the claim to the Pacific Ocean as indigenous homeland. The outrigger canoe enabled the settlement of the entire Pacific. In resistance contexts, it is the motif most d

motifs.name: Manulua
motifs.meaning: Unity, alliance, and the binding of social relationships. 'Two birds' — the paired forms represent unity and the binding of alliances between families and chiefs. A kupesi 'eiki (chiefly design) from

motifs.name: Masi Kesa
motifs.meaning: Clan identity, rank, and spiritual protection. Each sub-clan (tokatoka) owns specific masi motifs (draudrau) that are their intellectual property. The stencilling technique (kesakesa) is unique to Fij

motifs.name: Vonu
motifs.meaning: Longevity, endurance, navigation, and the connection between land and sea. The vonu is a symbol of wisdom and the link between the terrestrial (vanua) and oceanic realms. Key symbol in Pacific climate

motifs.name: Yvy Mara Ey
motifs.meaning: The mythical Guaraní paradise — a land free of hunger, war, and disease. The soul must achieve a state of perfection (aguyjé) to reach it. Associated with eastward migration, prophetic leadership, and

motifs.name: Cuaracy Ra Angaba
motifs.meaning: An astronomical stone calendar used by the Guaraní to track seasons, solstices, moon phases, and cardinal directions. 'Cuaracy' means 'sun' and 'Ra'angaba' means 'image.' The sun is the greatest deity

motifs.name: Nanduti
motifs.meaning: Traditional Paraguayan needle lace whose name means 'spider web' in Guaraní. Created by mestizo Guaraní women blending European needle-lace technique with indigenous craft. The spider-web form echoes

motifs.name: Kene Shipibo
motifs.meaning: The Shipibo-Conibo traditional geometric designs painted on ceramics, textiles, and bodies. They express the cosmological worldview: the universe is structured by intricate vibrational patterns first

motifs.name: Ronin Kuin
motifs.meaning: The primordial anaconda that, according to Shipibo-Conibo cosmology, sang the universe into existence. The pattern of her skin became the template for all kené designs. She is the 'mother of designs'

motifs.name: Mola Guna
motifs.meaning: Hand-stitched textile panels worn by Guna women of the San Blas Archipelago (Guna Yala), Panama. Its designs may be residuals of pre-Hispanic body-painting patterns. Traditional abstract designs are b

motifs.name: Jagua Pyta
motifs.meaning: In Guaraní cosmology, the Blue Jaguar (Jaguarový) is contained inside Nhamandu's hut at Yvy Marã Eÿ. If released, it would destroy humanity. The Red Jaguar is a guardian force. The jaguar motif in bod

motifs.name: Kayapo Jaguar Division
motifs.meaning: The jaguar is the apex predator and spiritual power animal of Kayapó cosmology. Jaguar-pattern body paint marks warriors, chiefs, and ceremony participants. Bilateral body division into upper (red, vi

motifs.name: Yanomami Urihi
motifs.meaning: Urihi a ('forest-land') is the Yanomami concept for the inhabited world — the second lowest of four cosmic layers. Davi Kopenawa's cosmology, recorded in 'The Falling Sky,' describes the entire cosmos

motifs.name: Luta Pela Vida
motifs.meaning: The Luta pela Vida (Struggle for Life) camp by APIB in Brasília (August–September 2021) was the largest indigenous mobilisation in Brazilian history. Body paint was simultaneously an act of cultural a

modules.title: What is Data? Indigenous Knowledges and the Digital World
modules.care: C (Collective Benefit) — data ecosystems must serve Indigenous peoples

modules.title: Data Colonialism: Who Took Our Data?
modules.care: A (Authority to Control) — recognising rights and interests in data

modules.title: CARE & OCAP: Frameworks for Data Sovereignty
modules.care: Full CARE framework — all four principles

modules.title: Land, Territory, and Digital Sovereignty
modules.care: R (Responsibility) — for positive relationships and expanding capability

modules.title: Building Indigenous AI: Resistance and Future Visions
modules.care: E (Ethics) — minimising harm, justice, and future use
